description_long: This object depicts a small suburban neighborhood block featuring six individual houses arranged in two parallel rows, each with its own driveway and surrounded by green lawns and scattered trees. The houses are simple, box-like structures with gable roofs, and most are painted in light colors, while two are darker. The layout includes paved driveways leading to each house, and the area is landscaped with numerous trees, providing a sense of privacy and greenery. The roofs are pitched, and the houses appear to be single-story, typical of ranch-style suburban homes. The scene is set on a rectangular plot of land, with the houses evenly spaced and oriented toward a central access road. The overall impression is of a quiet, planned residential neighborhood.
description: A suburban block with six single-story, gable-roofed houses, each with driveways and surrounded by trees on a grassy plot.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the front of the neighborhood, highlighting the driveways, entryways, and the arrangement of houses with trees scattered throughout.
description_view_1: View 1 provides a side perspective, emphasizing the depth of the plot and the alignment of houses and trees along the length of the block.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the rear of the houses, showing the backyards and the even distribution of trees behind the homes.
description_view_3: View 3 offers an angled view from the opposite end, focusing on the driveways and the open grassy area at the front of the block.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple front doors with short driveways, no visible porches or columns, minimal decorative elements.
